solution:
def longestDecomposition(self, text: str) -> int:
    k = len(set(text))
    return k

efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW

Explanation:
The problem requires finding the length of the longest substring that can be decomposed into k substrings, where each substring is a unique character from the given string. We can solve this problem by converting the string into a set, which automatically removes duplicate characters and stores only unique characters. The length of the set represents the number of substrings k, which is the longest possible decomposition.
